source: SESSION-HANDOFF.md, git log 2026-04-20..2026-05-01, script/talk-framework-v4.md → v6.md diffs
generated: hand-curated from real cut commits + handoff notes

intro.title: What got cut from the talk.
intro.lede: Every keynote is the keep pile sitting on top of the refuse pile. Below: the slides, lines, and whole story arcs that didn't make it into the May 1 talk — and why each one was thrown out. Filter by reason to see the pattern.
intro.pull: What you call artistic instinct? That's encoded choice. Decades on the cutting room floor, compressed into a sensibility.

reasons.label: Biographical padding
reasons.blurb: True about Kris but doesn't earn its 90 seconds in a 20-minute room.

reasons.label: Doesn't fit theme
reasons.blurb: A real story that bends the spine of the talk in the wrong direction.

reasons.label: Too long
reasons.blurb: Trim for pacing — the point survives compressed or implied.

reasons.label: Save for later
reasons.blurb: Held back for the book, the workshop, the next talk.

reasons.label: Duplicate beat
reasons.blurb: Same idea already lands earlier or later — kill the second pass.

reasons.label: Wrong frame
reasons.blurb: The argument was right; the framing reinforced what we wanted to refuse.

cuts.title: Open Everything (Bryght / Rain City / Dead.net)
cuts.body: Standalone slide on Kris's open-source years — Drupal, Rain City Studios, building the Grateful Dead community site. The thesis was "value lives in relationship, not locked files." Strong line, strong era, but a full slide of late-90s / early-00s biography on its own arc.
cuts.rescued_to: Thesis line preserved verbatim in the AI Chapter section; "this story is not new" transition folded into the Dada opener.

cuts.title: Spark Online (1998 web magazine)
cuts.body: Slide on the 1998 web publication Kris ran — Columbia interviews, "exploring electronic consciousness," the early web as a site of genuine intellectual experiment before the platforms ate it.
cuts.rescued_to: Nothing structural lost. The biographical thread now moves Cult Baby → Camera → 145K straight into the pattern section.

cuts.title: The Junior Pipeline (talk slide)
cuts.body: Standalone slide voicing the honest discomfort: "the answer isn't 'don't use the tools.' And the junior pipeline is a generation of creative people trying to figure out if there's a rung on the ladder for them. We owe them honesty, not reassurance."
cuts.rescued_to: Lives on as the /widgets/junior-pipeline diagram — full diagnosis with more room than the stage allows.

cuts.title: True North (2010 Olympics warehouse)
cuts.body: The 2010 Vancouver Olympics moment: no press credentials, made our own, bloggers and veteran photojournalists in the same warehouse, nimble while mainstream media drowned in bureaucracy. "That's the move. Not waiting for permission."
cuts.rescued_to: The "don't wait for permission" beat is already carried by Dada / détournement / Anthony Joseph's chainsaw line. The Olympics anecdote moves to /lineage as a node on the punk timeline.

cuts.title: The Best Tool (Killer Photos book)
cuts.body: The riff on Kris's iPhone book: "The best camera is the one you have with you. The perfect tool you don't use has produced exactly zero work. Same principle. Don't wait for the ethical perfect AI."
cuts.rescued_to: Echoed implicitly in Both Hands Full and the Boosters/Doomers slide — the "both statements are true" frame does the same job in fewer beats.

cuts.title: What Did You Throw Away (standalone slide)
cuts.body: A whole slide built around the question "what did you throw away this week?" with audience reflection time. Powerful prompt, asks for a beat the room may not give a stranger 14 minutes in.
cuts.rescued_to: Lives full-strength as the /widgets/taste-audit interactive — the question stays in the talk as a single line inside the cutting-room beat.

cuts.title: Cluster F — Kevin Friel full story
cuts.body: Extended Kevin Friel block: the pixel-wizard origin, the friendship arc, the late-night DM that became the closing line. A full story cluster, not just the closer quote.
cuts.rescued_to: Kevin's closing line — "Let's use these tools to f*** it up, y'all" — stays in the Close. The full story moves to source-material/kevin-friel-pixel-wizard for the book.

cuts.title: Matlock as the opening hook
cuts.body: Earlier draft opened with the Matlock anecdote — a measured, narrative-led entry into the AI argument. Linear four-act structure built on top of it.
cuts.rescued_to: Replaced by "1,800 photos" — a punk-tempo open that puts Kris's body of work on the table in the first ten seconds. The frame matches the talk's argument.

cuts.title: Linear four-act structure
cuts.body: The whole talk used to be Setup → Inciting Incident → Crisis → Resolution. Tidy, screenwriter-friendly, easy to outline.
cuts.rescued_to: Replaced by modular truth-bomb clusters. The form should match the argument: punk doesn't run in three acts.

cuts.title: Pattern / corpus story (slide 9)
cuts.body: A standalone slide unpacking the "the model is your corpus" beat with a worked example — what the prompt gets wrong, what the dataset gets wrong, where taste enters.
cuts.rescued_to: Moved to the Three Documents slide — the corpus argument is the reason to write the documents in the first place.

cuts.title: Both Hands Full as a single climax slide
cuts.body: Earlier deck had Both Hands Full as one slide doing two jobs at once — the cultural frame and the personal testimony jammed into the same beat.
cuts.rescued_to: Split into two: Both Hands Full (frame) and More Creative Than Ever (personal testimony). Each gets to land.

cuts.title: Open-questions monologue (closing)
cuts.body: Two-minute closing block walking the audience through the things Kris doesn't have answers for — pipeline, consent, climate, attribution. Honest, uncomfortable, slow.
cuts.rescued_to: Lives as the /decisions page on the companion site — full text, sourced, browsable. The talk closes faster.
